---
title: "Data Acquisition Project"
author: "Nandintsetseg Batsaikhan"
date: "2026-03-11"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
---

##Building a Spotify Song Dataset with the Spotify API
Introduction

Music streaming platforms generate enormous amounts of data about songs, artists, and listening behavior. Spotify in particular provides a rich developer API that allows researchers and developers to access information about tracks, albums, and artists.

For this project, I created an original dataset of Spotify tracks using the Spotify Web API. The goal was to practice collecting real-world data using APIs and to construct a dataset that could later be used for exploratory analysis or modeling.

Many students in data science work with pre-existing datasets, but collecting data directly from an API provides a better understanding of how real datasets are generated. APIs also introduce practical considerations such as rate limits, authentication, and data cleaning.

This project demonstrates how to use the Spotify API to programmatically gather song information and convert the raw API responses into a structured dataset.

Motivating Question

The motivating question behind this project is:

What characteristics of songs appear in Spotify’s catalog, and how might they relate to song popularity or listening trends?

Although this post focuses on dataset creation rather than full analysis, the dataset could later be used to explore questions such as:

Are longer songs less popular?

Do certain artists dominate search results?

Are particular albums more frequently represented?

What patterns appear across song metadata such as duration or explicit content?

Creating a dataset of songs allows us to begin exploring these types of questions.
